completed_phases.name: Delivery Foundation Bootstrap
completed_phases.summary: Bootstrapped the structured docs tree, persistent roadmap state, CI and release workflow scaffolding, Terraform scaffolding, and a Docusaurus site scaffold.

completed_phases.name: macOS Session Recovery Hardening
completed_phases.summary: Preserved finished recordings when transcription cannot start because the OpenAI key is missing, invalid, or revoked at stop time, kept the session retryable in the library, and added regression coverage for retry success.

completed_phases.name: Accessibility Audit And Interaction Hardening
completed_phases.summary: Hardened the macOS menu bar, recording controls, session library, extracted-issue review, and settings surfaces with clearer accessibility labels, selected-state semantics, keyboard affordances, and updated accessibility documentation.

completed_phases.name: Product Spec Consolidation
completed_phases.summary: Created a canonical BugNarrator product spec, clarified the authority split between product behavior, roadmap state, and release history, and updated maintainer and Windows docs to reference the spec directly.

completed_phases.name: Recovery Surface Polish
completed_phases.summary: Surfaced retry-needed sessions in the menu bar window and at the top of the session-library list, and added retry counts to library summaries so preserved-session recovery stays visible after relaunch.

completed_phases.name: Accessibility Automation Seed
completed_phases.summary: Added a lightweight accessibility regression script, wired it into CI and release workflow scaffolding, and documented the boundary between the new automation seed and the remaining live assistive-technology validation work.

completed_phases.name: Spec-Driven Parity Matrix
completed_phases.summary: Created a cross-platform parity matrix and linked macOS, Windows, and architecture docs back to named product-spec contracts instead of ad hoc parity notes.

completed_phases.name: Docs Site Sync And Build Validation
completed_phases.summary: Synced the docs site to the canonical onboarding, user, and product-spec content, fixed the site shell and favicon configuration, generated a lockfile, and validated a strict Docusaurus production build locally.

completed_phases.name: Release Summary Automation Seed
completed_phases.summary: Added an internal release-summary generator based on the changelog and roadmap state, surfaced it in the release workflow as a run artifact and step summary, and documented it as a maintainer aid rather than a public release-note replacement.

completed_phases.name: Dependency Alert Remediation
completed_phases.summary: Remediated both default-branch Dependabot alert sets, replaced the broken local docs-site npm path with a pinned Node 22 wrapper, added runtime-guardrails regression coverage for the FAIL, NOT RUN, and missing-state-update rules, and verified that the default branch returned no open Dependabot alerts after PR #6 and PR #8 merged.

upcoming_phases.name: Docs Site Publication
scope: wire hosting or deployment for the site

upcoming_phases.name: Release Summary Promotion
scope: promote the internal release-summary seed into a maintainer-reviewed release note draft flow
decide how much roadmap state belongs in public release notes
attach release-summary generation to the release checklist

upcoming_phases.name: Retry-Needed Session Filter
scope: add a dedicated retry-needed session-library filter or saved view
keep recovery discovery visible in larger histories
consider explicit retry-needed counts in date-filter metadata

risk_remediation_phases.name: Windows Runtime Validation And Hardening
risk_remediation_phases.scope: Validate Milestones 2-4 on a real Windows machine or VM and fix tray, recording, and screenshot runtime issues discovered there.

risk_remediation_phases.name: Assistive Technology Runtime And Docs Validation
risk_remediation_phases.scope: Run a real VoiceOver-driven validation pass on the release-candidate macOS UI and validate docs-site accessibility once the Docusaurus site is published.

opportunities.description: Publish the validated Docusaurus docs site and keep a hosted smoke-check flow once deployment is wired.
Expand CI to include release-style artifact validation and richer Windows runtime confidence once runner support is proven.
Promote the new internal release-summary seed into a maintainer-reviewed public release-note drafting flow.
Add a dedicated retry-needed filter or saved view so preserved-session recovery stays easy to find in larger histories.

risks.description: The repo was missing structured delivery docs, persistent roadmap state, CI/release workflow files, infra scaffolding, and a docs-site scaffold.
risks.impact: Future phases, release decisions, and remediation planning would drift because key delivery state lived only in ad hoc notes.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in PF-001 by creating the structured docs tree, state.json, workflow scaffolding, Terraform scaffold, and site scaffold.

risks.description: If the OpenAI key is removed or invalidated during a recording, the finished audio can be lost when transcription cannot start at stop time.
risks.impact: Users can lose a completed recording session and have to re-record important testing evidence.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in RR-001 by preserving finished audio inside session artifacts, persisting retryable session metadata in the library, and allowing transcription retry after the key is restored or replaced.

risks.description: Windows tray shell, recording, and screenshot workflows have been scaffolded but not yet validated on a real Windows machine or VM.
risks.impact: The Windows workspace may compile conceptually while failing in real tray, permission, or capture behavior.
risks.mitigation: Track and fix in RR-002. This phase now improves CI and packaged artifact validation, but real Windows desktop validation is still required before the risk can be closed.

risks.description: CI does not yet prove full Windows runtime behavior or fully prove signed release automation.
risks.impact: Build confidence is improving, but key Windows and release-time behaviors can still regress outside local maintainer workflows.
risks.mitigation: Track and improve in RR-002 and OPS-002. RR-002 now adds phase-branch CI coverage, explicit Windows test-project execution, packaged zip validation, packaged-app smoke execution, packaged-file hash parity checks, and uploaded validation evidence, but signed release proof and real runtime behavior are still unresolved.

risks.description: GitHub Actions workflow runtimes needed hosted validation after the Node24 compatibility upgrade because the initial hosted Release run still surfaced a Node 20 deprecation annotation from actions/setup-python@v5.
risks.impact: CI and release evidence could still degrade under the hosted-runner Node 24 cutover unless every action pin was validated on GitHub-hosted runners.
risks.mitigation: Resolved by running GitHub-hosted CI run 23988211616 and unsigned Release workflow run 23988213079 on commit eca6ba9 after upgrading actions/setup-python to v6; both workflows completed successfully without the prior Node 20 deprecation annotation.

risks.description: The Windows service layer contained ambiguous string-splitting calls that prevented the Windows-targeted test project from compiling when RR-002 validation began exercising it directly.
risks.impact: Windows validation could fail before runtime checks even started, hiding real desktop issues behind avoidable compile errors.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in RR-002 by replacing the ambiguous collection-expression separators with explicit character arrays in Windows settings and session-bundle export code.

risks.description: Inherited validation defects caused RR-002 macOS and Windows CI signal to fail before the phase branch could prove the intended package and smoke checks.
risks.impact: Release smoke and packaged-app validation could fail for unrelated reasons, masking the remaining real Windows runtime risk behind broken validation plumbing.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in RR-002 by redacting exact sensitive metadata values from diagnostics free-form text and by capturing packaged smoke exit codes with Start-Process -PassThru -Wait in the Windows package validator.

risks.description: The live app still lacks a complete accessibility and 508-style interaction audit across keyboard flow, focus management, and assistive-technology behavior.
risks.impact: Accessibility regressions could ship unnoticed because most validation is still manual and scenario-based.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in RR-003 by hardening keyboard-first controls, explicit labels and selected-state semantics, settings-field labeling, transient toast announcements, and accessibility-specific QA/testing documentation for the current macOS app surfaces.

risks.description: A real VoiceOver-driven runtime pass and docs-site accessibility validation still have not been executed on a published docs site or release-candidate macOS build.
risks.impact: Semantic hardening is in place, but live assistive-technology behavior and hosted docs accessibility can still regress without an end-to-end manual validation pass.
risks.mitigation: Track in RR-005 and complete once the docs site is published and the next release candidate is ready for a live assistive-technology pass.

risks.description: The repo still lacks a single canonical BugNarrator product spec document, even though multiple docs and prompts refer to one as a source of truth.
risks.impact: Platform plans, user docs, and future prompts can drift because the product contract is distributed across multiple files instead of one maintained spec.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in RR-004 by creating docs/architecture/product-spec.md as the canonical product contract and updating roadmap, release, maintainer, and Windows docs to reference it directly instead of an implied spec or the current macOS implementation.

risks.description: Local DMG packaging could bypass the release smoke and startup keychain regression checks because scripts/build_dmg.sh built and packaged the app without enforcing scripts/release_smoke_test.sh first.
risks.impact: A startup keychain-prompt regression could slip into candidate DMG artifacts even if the lower-level fix and unit tests existed, because the packaging entrypoint itself was not proving the symptom path.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in OPS-011 by making scripts/build_dmg.sh require release_smoke_test.sh by default, which now includes the startup keychain smoke, and by rerunning unsigned DMG packaging through that hardened path.

risks.description: Local DMG packaging depended on the dmgbuild virtualenv entrypoint script, which could carry a stale shebang from another checkout and fail before producing release artifacts.
risks.impact: Local release preparation could fail before producing any DMG artifacts, blocking maintainers from validating or packaging candidate builds.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in OPS-011 by making scripts/build_dmg.sh invoke dmgbuild through the active virtualenv Python module instead of the fragile bin/dmgbuild wrapper.

risks.description: GitHub reported four open Dependabot alerts on the default branch during push operations on 2026-04-04 (2 high, 2 moderate).
risks.impact: Open dependency vulnerabilities can leave the repo and release workflows exposed until the affected packages or action pins are remediated and revalidated.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in OPS-012 by merging PR #6 into main at merge commit dc7a66aa7b2fc42a0b05edddec6169c0f6a88135 and rechecking the GitHub Dependabot alert API, which no longer showed the original four default-branch alerts on 2026-04-04.

risks.description: After the first default-branch remediation landed, GitHub still reported two open lodash Dependabot alerts on site/package-lock.json on 2026-04-04 (1 high, 1 moderate).
risks.impact: The default branch remains exposed to the remaining lodash vulnerabilities until the follow-up dependency remediation is validated and promoted.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in OPS-012 by validating the lodash 4.18.1 remediation locally and on hosted CI run 23995836693, merging PR #8 into main at fa6f96798796f1d6cc42c4d7c05482abd4f6393c, and confirming the default-branch Dependabot alert API returned no open alerts on 2026-04-05.

risks.description: The local npm 11.12.1 / Node 25.9.0 toolchain can fail registry fetches with EBADF during docs-site dependency remediation, preventing a normal npm install or lockfile refresh path in this macOS workspace.
risks.impact: Security or dependency updates can stall locally even when the registry is reachable, forcing manual tarball-based workarounds and reducing confidence in repeatable package-management flows.
risks.mitigation: Resolved in OPS-012 by adding site/.node-version plus scripts/site_npm.sh, which downloads and reuses the current official Node 22 toolchain under build/tooling and runs docs-site npm commands through that pinned path instead of the broken system npm 11 / Node 25 combination.

incidents.description: Phase bootstrap detected missing persistent state, structured delivery docs, workflow automation files, and infrastructure scaffolding required for disciplined multi-phase delivery.
RR-001 confirmed that a completed recording could be discarded when transcription could not start because the OpenAI key was missing, invalid, or revoked at stop time.
incidents.remediation: Executed RR-001 macOS Session Recovery Hardening.

incidents.description: RR-003 found unlabeled extracted-issue export controls, weak selected-state semantics in custom session-library controls, and keyboard-only affordance gaps in the recording controls and settings flows.
incidents.remediation: Executed RR-003 Accessibility Audit And Interaction Hardening.

incidents.description: RR-004 confirmed that multiple maintainer, roadmap, and Windows docs referred to a BugNarrator spec as a source of truth even though no canonical product spec file existed in the repo.
Retry-needed sessions were preserved after RR-001, but they still depended too heavily on opening the session detail pane before the recovery state became obvious again.
The repo still lacked any automated regression tripwire for accessibility-sensitive macOS surfaces, leaving the RR-003 baseline fully dependent on manual review.
Cross-platform parity rules were documented in prose, but there was no single parity ledger that mapped named product-spec contracts to macOS and Windows status.
The Docusaurus site scaffold was not yet synced to the canonical structured docs and initially failed a strict production build because the site shell still linked to a missing homepage route.
incidents.remediation: Executed OPS-006 Docs Site Sync And Build Validation.

incidents.description: The release workflow had no internal seed artifact summarizing the current changelog and roadmap state, so maintainers still had to reconstruct that context manually during packaging.
incidents.remediation: Executed OPS-007 Release Summary Automation Seed.

current_phase.name: Windows Runtime Validation And Hardening
current_phase.summary: RR-002 is blocked on real Windows desktop runtime validation. The most recent completed work closed OPS-012 after PR #8 merged the remaining lodash remediation plus validator regression coverage into main and GitHub cleared the default-branch Dependabot alerts; the remaining active execution blocker is still RR-002-T4 on a real Windows machine or VM.

decisions.summary: Selected PF-001 Delivery Foundation Bootstrap as the next phase because persistent roadmap state, structured docs, CI/release workflow files, infra scaffold, and site scaffold were blocking dependencies.
Kept the application version at 1.0.21 because PF-001 is internal delivery-foundation work and is not being released as a new app build.
Preserved the current locally controlled signed-release process while adding GitHub workflow scaffolding, rather than claiming the new workflow is already the only production release path.
Selected RR-001 macOS Session Recovery Hardening next because RISK-MAC-001 was the highest-severity unresolved risk that was feasible to remediate in the current local macOS workspace.

Used the existing transcript library with explicit pending-transcription metadata for preserved sessions so the recovery path stays visible in the current review workflow without inventing a second recovery UI surface in this phase.
Removed preserved retry audio after a successful retry in normal mode to limit raw-audio retention while still keeping debug-mode preservation available for diagnostics.

Selected RR-003 Accessibility Audit And Interaction Hardening next because RR-002 requires real Windows runtime validation, while the accessibility risk was fully actionable in the current macOS workspace.

Closed the baseline accessibility remediation work in RR-003, but split the remaining live VoiceOver and hosted docs-site validation into RR-005 so residual assistive-technology risk stays explicitly planned.

Selected RR-004 Product Spec Consolidation next because RR-002 remains blocked on real Windows runtime validation, while the missing canonical product contract was fully actionable in the current workspace.

Established docs/architecture/product-spec.md as the canonical source of truth for product behavior, kept docs/roadmap/state.json as the planning and risk source of truth, and kept CHANGELOG.md as the shipped change-history source of truth.
Skipped RR-002 and RR-005 in this run because they still require real Windows runtime validation and a live assistive-technology pass that cannot be completed honestly in the current macOS workspace.

Completed the next five executable opportunity phases instead: OPS-003 through OPS-007, covering recovery surfacing, accessibility regression automation, parity tracking, docs-site sync/build validation, and release-summary seed tooling.

Kept OPS-003 narrowly scoped to menu bar and session-library visibility improvements instead of adding a new dedicated retry-needed filter in the same pass; the remaining filter idea is now planned as OPS-010.

Used a lightweight code-level accessibility regression script for OPS-004 and explicitly left live VoiceOver and hosted docs-site validation in RR-005 so the automation seed does not overclaim coverage.

Split the original docs-site and release-summary opportunity work into executable local phases first, then left site publication and public release-note promotion as explicit future phases after local validation passed.

Resumed RR-002 on a dedicated phase branch and scoped it to the feasible Windows confidence slice in this macOS workspace: phase-branch CI coverage, explicit Windows test-project execution, packaged zip validation, and headless packaged-app smoke execution, while leaving real Windows desktop runtime validation explicitly unresolved.

While exercising the Windows-targeted test project, fixed ambiguous string-splitting calls in the Windows service layer so RR-002 validation no longer fails at compile time before runtime checks begin.
Kept RR-002 on phase/RR-002-windows-runtime-hardening and cherry-picked only the targeted validation-fix commit so the phase record stays isolated from unrelated feature-branch work.

Treated the inherited diagnostics-redaction failure and packaged-smoke exit-code bug as in-scope RR-002 work because they blocked the package/runtime signal, then revalidated DiagnosticsLoggerTests and release_smoke_test.sh on the phase branch before updating state.

Expanded RR-002 CI evidence by validating packaged-file hashes against publish outputs, emitting a machine-readable Windows validation manifest, and uploading that manifest plus the smoke report as GitHub Actions artifacts from the passing phase-branch run.
Tracked the new GitHub Actions Node 20 deprecation warning as RISK-CI-002 in OPS-011 instead of folding that maintenance work into RR-002.

Added a repo-native Windows Codex handoff document and script that summarize the active RR-002 state, can run the scripted Windows baseline on a real Windows machine, and emit a machine-readable handoff report for takeover work.

Created a repo-local execution bootstrap on phase/bootstrap by adding reusable agent and prompt contracts, normalizing roadmap state with last_updated and task summaries, and making a single runtime guardrails validator the CI gatekeeper instead of introducing a parallel enforcement system.

Because RR-002 remains blocked on real Windows validation in this macOS workspace, used the next unblocked execution slice to advance OPS-011 by upgrading workflow action majors to Node24-compatible releases, forcing Node24 in CI and release workflows, and teaching the runtime guardrails validator to enforce that floor.

Treated the stale dmgbuild entrypoint shebang as a release-environment defect, switched scripts/build_dmg.sh to invoke dmgbuild through the active virtualenv Python module, reran release_smoke_test.sh, rebuilt the unsigned DMG successfully, and generated a fresh release-summary seed without claiming a public signed/notarized release.

Hardened the local packaging entrypoint by making scripts/build_dmg.sh run scripts/release_smoke_test.sh by default, which carries the startup keychain smoke into DMG preflight; then reran workflow lint and unsigned DMG packaging through that exact path before recording release status.

Closed OPS-011 after hosted CI run 23988211616 and hosted Release workflow run 23988213079 both succeeded on commit eca6ba9; upgraded actions/setup-python to v6 when the first hosted Release run still emitted a Node 20 deprecation annotation.

Started OPS-012 because RR-002 remains blocked on real Windows validation, patched the docs-site dependency tree to replace the four vulnerable Dependabot versions, validated the patched tree with npm ls plus a Docusaurus production build, and recorded the local npm 11 / Node 25 EBADF fetch defect as a separate OPS-012 environment risk.

Validated the OPS-012 dependency-remediation commit on hosted CI run 23988782016 after pushing phase/bootstrap, then kept the phase open only for default-branch promotion and the local npm 11 / Node 25 toolchain defect.

Closed OPS-012-T3 by replacing the broken system npm 11 / Node 25 docs-site install path with a repo-local Node 22 wrapper aligned to the docs-site CI runtime, then validated npm ci and the production site build through that pinned toolchain.

Promoted the original OPS-012 remediation to main via PR #6, verified the original four default-branch Dependabot alerts were gone, and closed that cleared alert set as RISK-SEC-001 instead of leaving it open after promotion.

Split the two newly surfaced lodash Dependabot alerts into a follow-up OPS-012 slice, pinned lodash to the non-deprecated patched release 4.18.1, and treated the initial hosted CI failure on commit 64633e9 as a guardrail-enforced state-sync miss rather than a code regression.

After syncing the canonical state files, reran hosted CI successfully on commit 40ddf31 and closed the tracked-branch validation step for the follow-up lodash remediation, leaving only the default-branch promotion and alert-closure confirmation open.

Added direct runtime-guardrails regression tests for the FAIL evidence, NOT RUN evidence, and missing-state-update rules before promoting the remaining lodash remediation, so the exact branch-blocking validator behavior is now covered by local test execution.

Merged PR #8 after hosted CI run 23995836693 passed and then confirmed the default-branch Dependabot alert API returned no open alerts, closing OPS-012 and leaving RR-002 as the only active blocked phase.
